title: Novo forsøger at vinde investorers tillid: To eks-investorer siger klart “nej tak”
summary_md:
# Novo forsøger at vinde investorers tillid: To eks-investorer siger klart “nej tak”

**Feed**: borsen-podcast  
**Published**: 2026-06-17  
**Market Sentiment**: Mixed

⏱️ **Time Horizon Key**: ST = <3 months | MT = 3-12 months | LT = >12 months

---

## Episode Thesis

This Danish-language episode debates whether fear or euphoria drives current markets amid Trump-era geopolitical volatility, warning that the record SpaceX IPO and Tesla represent speculative FOMO detached from earnings logic. Value investors Kurt Kare and Jens Løstrup favor cheap, cash-rich quality (Hewlett Packard, Berkshire Hathaway, Qualcomm, Jyske Bank, Torm) and frame equities as inflation protection against likely US financial repression. Hosts are skeptical of IPOs (Installatørgruppen, Biomar, Bara Juice) and discuss Novo Nordisk's struggle to win back investor trust.

---

## Universe Stocks

### 🟢 HPQ — Hewlett Packard | **BUY** (Strong)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 80%

PE 6, ~17% total shareholder yield, AI PC upgrade cycle; earnings could double over 5 years implying ~15%/yr return.

#### Reasoning

Kurt frames HP as a classic value play: PE ~6, ~9% dividend plus 6-7% buybacks buying back half the company over 5 years, doubling per-share earnings. Upcoming AI PC upgrade cycle is a tailwind; HP offers best hardware value proposition without Apple's brand premium. Main risk is rising RAM/hardware prices compressing margins, but he believes that is priced in.

#### Sell Triggers

- if RAM/hardware prices keep rising and compress margins further
- if the AI PC upgrade cycle fails to materialize
- if buyback pace slows materially

> *"Hewlett Packard är ju en klassisk köd- och kartoffel- och value-aktie den handlar price-earning 6 och utbetalar omkring jag tror det är 9% i utbyte och laver aktier tillbaka i köp för 6-7%"*

---

### 🟢 BRK-B — Berkshire Hathaway | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 60%

Operating businesses at effective PE ~6 plus ~$400bn cash; cash optionality and quality act as a hedge if markets fall.

#### Reasoning

Kurt holds Berkshire opportunistically as a value case and market hedge: strip out ~$400bn cash and the operating businesses trade at PE ~6. New old-school CEO and Buffett's heavy selling leave it positioned to buy in a downturn. Jens agrees it is quality plus cash, well-placed if markets fall, but notes the risk that Buffett mis-timed by selling so much if markets keep rising.

#### Sell Triggers

- if markets keep rising and the cash drag persists
- if the company fails to deploy cash at attractive valuations
- concerns over Buffett's health/succession materialize negatively

> *"Berkshire för mig det är lite en hedge mot om det är går ting gal på markedet det är en value case som sagt jag får datorsällskaperna till sex gånger intjäningen och så har man 400 miljarder dollar i kontanter"*

---

### 🟢 QCOM — Qualcomm | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 60%

Makes neural processing units; positioned for the coming household-robot era ('robots' iPhone moment').

#### Reasoning

Kurt holds Qualcomm as a long-term play on the next computing shift: it makes neural processing units, and he expects household robots (~SEK80-100k) within three years to become 'the new car sector', driving demand for its chips. Speculative on timing but grounded in the AI/robotics thesis.

#### Sell Triggers

- if the household-robot adoption timeline slips well beyond three years
- if NPU demand fails to ramp
- if competition erodes Qualcomm's position

> *"Qualcomm laver så små det som heter neural processing units för vi är på väg mot en tid där jag tror det nästa som kommer det är robotarnas iPhone-öjeblick"*

---

### 🟢 JYSK — Jyske Bank | **BUY** (Strong)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 85%

PE ~10, ~6% earnings yield, record DKK3bn buyback (~5.5% of shares), low free float; half of earnings from growing mortgage credit.

#### Reasoning

Jens bought massively in 2020-21 at ~0.4x book and continues to hold as one of the cheapest large Danish stocks on every metric. The cocktail of a low valuation plus aggressive buybacks (record DKK3bn, ~5.5% of shares) compounds per-share value, amplified by low free float (~30% held by a foundation). Rising rates and a strong housing market should lift mortgage-credit lending, half of earnings. Has delivered ~35.5% annual return over five years.

#### Sell Triggers

- if rates fall sharply, pressuring net interest income on deposits
- if the housing market and mortgage lending weaken
- if the buyback program is curtailed
- financial repression depressing bank balance sheets

> *"i min bok är det en av de billigaste aktier i de stora danska aktier överhuvudet på alla nöjltal och därför håller jag fast och vi kan se i övrigt ett historiskt högt aktietillbaka program med 3 miljarder"*

---

### 🟡 TRMD-A — Torm | **HOLD** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 60%

Cheaply priced product tanker; portfolio hedge that rises on conflict; expects very high dividends (~5-9% of price per quarter) over coming quarters.

#### Reasoning

Jens holds Torm (~0.2%) as a counter-balance: it rises during conflicts (Ukraine, Iran) due to oil/freight disruption. He doesn't expect the share to rise much as it's already richly priced for earnings, but anticipates a 'rain of dividends' — a very high payout (~DKK11-18) tied to Q2, ~5-9% of price in one quarter. High freight rates should persist as depleted global oil reserves are refilled, sustaining strong earnings.

#### Sell Triggers

- if freight rates normalize after reserves are refilled
- if geopolitical tensions ease, reducing oil disruption
- if the high dividend proves unsustainable beyond a quarter or two

> *"vi kommer till att se ett pengaregn i form av några stora utbytter jag förväntar en riktigt hög utbyte här i förbindelse med med anden kvartal"*

---

### 🟡 9984 — Softbank | **HOLD** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 70%

Bought 2022 at 40% of NAV; owns ARM and OpenAI stake; example of buying when unpopular as AI thesis played out.

#### Reasoning

Kurt cites Softbank as a value win bought in 2022 at 40% of estimated NAV when CEO Masa Son was mocked on the FT front page over WeWork's $3.5bn loss. Its private-equity AI focus (ARM, OpenAI) paid off as AI proved real and transformative. Held as illustration of buying quality when unpopular.

#### Sell Triggers

- if AI portfolio valuations correct sharply
- if NAV discount closes fully
- if private-equity bets sour

> *"vi köpte faktiskt ett japanskt sällskap som heter Softbank som i 2022 handlade till 40% av deras beregnade intervärdi"*

---

### ⛔ AAPL — Apple | **AVOID** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Negative | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 50%

Called 'the new IBM' — behind on AI, coasting on its name and brand premium.

#### Reasoning

Kurt is skeptical of Apple, calling it 'the new IBM': behind on AI and living on its brand. He contrasts it unfavorably with HP, which delivers value without charging double for a fruit logo. A soft negative view rather than an active short thesis.

#### Sell Triggers

- if Apple catches up meaningfully on AI
- if it demonstrates renewed product innovation

> *"Apple är i min optik är Apple den nya IBM De är de är bagud"*

---

### ⛔ TSLA — Tesla | **AVOID** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Mixed | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 55%

Detached from normal valuation (~350x earnings); pure momentum on Musk following, not earnings-driven.

#### Reasoning

Tesla is described as a fantastic past return but disconnected from normal valuation logic, trading at roughly 350 years to earn back the investment. Hosts say it runs on pure momentum and the 'quasi-religious' Musk following rather than a business engine generating value, so it could keep running but is not a value-based buy.

#### Sell Triggers

- if Musk-driven momentum fades
- if the market re-anchors valuation to fundamentals
- if robot/AI ambitions disappoint

> *"Tesla är ju också lösredet från den allmänliga prissättning på aktiemarknaden. Sist jag kikade på den då var det så här 350 år att tjäna en investering"*

---
